title: version-gating on deserialize (loud override)
manager: When you deserialize a machine snapshot, FSL checks the FSL version it was written with and refuses — loudly, with an explicit override — to load something newer than the runtime understands. This is the seatbelt on persistence: a v6 runtime silently misreading a v9 snapshot is a corruption bug, so gating turns it into a clear "this snapshot is from a newer FSL; here is how to force it if you truly mean to."
cs: version check on deserialize; refuse newer-than-runtime with a loud explicit override (force flag); fsl#1057 (serialize refuses newer) / #1056 (deserialize re-verifies) / #1017 (force flag).
